background.occupation: Marketing Analytics Manager at a mid-sized tech company
background.social_class: Middle Class (urban professional, rents apartment, has student debt)
background.key_experience: In 2020, her company forced a return-to-office policy after two years of successful remote work, claiming it was for 'collaboration,' but Chloe noticed productivity metrics actually dropped 15% afterward. She became convinced corporate leadership prioritizes control over actual results.

dominant_traits: Very High Neuroticism (constantly anxious about job security)
Very Low Openness to Experience (resists new workflows unless mandated)

cognition.core_value: Data should always trump corporate tradition
cognition.bias: Confirmation bias - aggressively searches for metrics supporting remote work benefits after seeing any pro-office article, ignoring contradictory studies

current_state.recent_memory: Her CEO gave a town hall speech yesterday praising 'synergy from watercooler chats' while quarterly reports showed declining innovation metrics
